DAMAGE OCCURED TO A HOME AND ADJACENT SHED WHEN AN OAK TREE WAS UPROOTED. ALSO, MULTIPLE OTHER TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. DAMAGE WAS CAUSED BY STRAIGHT LINE WINDS. DAMAGE PATH WAS APPROXIMATELY 500 FT LONG AND WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED BETWEEN 75 AND 80 MPH.

MULTIPLE PINE TREES WERE UPROOTED ADJACENT TO A HOME ON NEW OAK GROVE ROAD. ONE OF THE TREES FELL ON AND DESTROYED A CARPORT. THE DAMAGE WAS CAUSED BY STRAIGHT LINE WINDS WHICH WERE ESTIMATED TO BE BETWEEN 70 AND 75 MPH.

A TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN IN THE ROCK CREEK AREA NEAR GLAZE ROAD AND THE ROCK CREEK BAPTIST CHURCH. DAMAGE WAS LIMITED TO A SMALL GROVE OF SNAPPED AND UPROOTED PINE TREES. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 200 YARDS WITH A WIDTH OF 30 YARDS. THE TORNADO HAS BEEN RATED AND EF-0 ON THE ENHANCED FUJITA SCALE AS DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT WITH WINDS OF 65 TO 75 MPH.

SPORADIC UPROOTED AND SNAPPED TREES WERE FOUND ALONG A 4.5 MILE DISTANCE. THE DAMAGE WAS CAUSED BY STRAIGHT LINE WINDS. SOME OF THE DOWNED TREES FELL ON HOUSES AND OUT BUILDINGS CAUSING STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. SOME LIGHT/MINOR ROOF AND STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS NOTED IN THE FORM OF SHINGLE AND SIDING DAMAGE. IN THE COLONIAL GRAND APARTMENT COMPLEX, MULITPLE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST AND CAUSED DAMAGE IN THE CEDAR BROOK APARTMENT COMPLEX. MULTIPLE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND MAJOR ROOF DAMAGE OCCURRED WHEN A BRICK FIRE WALL USED TO SEPARATE UNITS COLLAPSED. THE PATH LENGTH OF THE BRIEF TORNADO WAS APPROXIMATELY 2000 FT LONG AND 75 FEET WIDE. THE TORNADO HAS BEEN RATED AN EF-0 ON THE ENHANCED FUJITA SCALE AS DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT WITH WINDS OF 75 TO 80 MPH.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON BEREA ROAD IN NORTHEAST FAYETTE COUNTY AND CONTINUED ALONG COUNTY ROAD 24 UNTIL MOVING INTO WALKER COUNTY. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN OR SNAPPED. ONE HOUSE RECEIVED SIGNIFICANT STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WHEN THE ENTIRE ROOF SHIFTED. THE TORNADO WAS RATED AN EF-1 WITH PEAK WINDS OF 100 MPH AND A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 100 YARDS.
2 HOMES DAMAGED IN THE COMMUNITY OF BAZEMORE.

A TORNADO CROSSED THE FAYETTE AND WALKER COUNTY LINE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 24 AND HIGHWAY 13. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST ACROSS WALKER COUNTY AND HIT THE TOWN OF KANSAS BEFORE LIFTING. MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS IN THE TOWN OF KANSAS. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED. THE TREES WERE OVER 100 YEARS OLD WITH THE LARGEST TRUNK 4 FEET IN DIAMETER. THE TORNADO WAS RATED AN EF-1 WITH PEAK WINDS OF 100 MPH AND MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 50 YARDS.

A TORNADO CROSSED THE FAYETTE AND WALKER COUNTY LINE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 24 AND HIGHWAY 13. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST ACROSS WALKER COUNTY AND HIT THE TOWN OF KANSAS BEFORE LIFTING. MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS IN THE TOWN OF KANSAS. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED. THE TREES WERE OVER 100 YEARS OLD WITH THE LARGEST TRUNK 4 FEET IN DIAMETER. THE TORNADO WAS RATED AN EF-1 WITH PEAK WINDS OF 100 MPH AND MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 50 YARDS.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTHEAST OF THE MARYLEE COMMUNITY, IN EXTREME NORTHEAST WALKER COUNTY. FROM THERE IT MOVED EASTWARD, ACROSS SMITH LAKE, AND CONTINUED INTO CULLMAN COUNTY. IN WALKER COUNTY, THIS TORNADO DAMAGED AT LEAST 5 CHICKEN HOUSES, BLEW DOWN HUNDREDS OF TREES, AND SIGNIFICANTLY DAMAGED AT LEAST 5 BOAT HOUSES ON SMITH LAKE. DAMAGE RATING IN WALKER COUNTY WAS EF-1, WITH PEAK WINDS AROUND 105 MPH.

A ROUGHLY 7 MILE LONG AND AS MUCH AS 3 MILE WIDE SWATH OF DAMAGE WAS FOUND IN THE SOUTHEASTERN SECTION OF THE CITY OF MONTGOMERY. THE CENTER LINE OF THE DAMAGE BEGAN NEAR HUNTINGTON COLLEGE, BETWEEN I-85 AND US-80, AND MOVED SOUTHEASTWARD FROM THERE TO JUST BEYOND THE INTERSECTION OF AL-271 AND US-231. THE MOST INTENSE DAMAGE WAS FOUND NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF US-80 AND US-231. WINDS IN THIS AREA WERE ESTIMATED AROUND 100 MPH.
THOUSANDS OF TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN OR WERE SNAPPED OFF ALONG THE DAMAGE PATH. SEVERAL MOBILE HOMES SUFFERED DAMAGE AND ONE CARPORT WAS DAMAGED. PATH LENGTH WAS AROUND 25 MILES.
TREES HAVE BEEN BLOWN DOWN NEAR THE SCHOTSVILLE COMMUNITY.

NUMEROUS TREES WERE EITHER SNAPPED OFF OR UPROOTED ALONG THE PATH. SEVERAL STRUCTURES SUFFERED VARYING DEGREES OF DAMAGE. DAMAGE PATH BEGAN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF BIRDFARM ROAD AND SNOW LAKE ROAD AND ENDED NEAR THE CULLMAN COUNTY LINE. EF-1 TORNADO...WITH ESTIMATED WINDS AROUND 95 MPH. THE TORNADO AFFECTED AREAS NEAR THE SUNLIGHT, BURROWS CROSSROADS, AND POWELLVILLE COMMUNITIES.

THE TORNADO APPEARED TO HAVE CAUSED ONLY ONLY MINOR TREE DAMAGE IN WALKER COUNTY. THIS STORM STREGTHENED AND PRODUCED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE IN CULLMAN COUNTY. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN EAST OF COUNTY ROAD 77 NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF GRAVLEE JUNCTION ROAD AND COUNTY ROAD 15. THE STORM ENTERED CULLMAN COUNTY NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 17 AND COUNTY ROAD 19. EF-0 DAMAGE IN WALKER COUNTY WITH WINDS LESS THAN 85 MPH...BUT EF-2 OVERALL...WITH WINDS UP TO 120 MPH IN CULLMAN COUNTY.

A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF KENDRICK ROAD AND DOGTOWN ROAD. THE TORNADO HAS BEEN RATED AN EF0. ONE CHICKEN HOUSE SUSTAINED MAJOR DAMAGE, A FEW TREE TOPS WERE SNAPPED OFF, TWO OUTBUILDINGS SUFFERED MINOR DAMAGE, AND A FEW TREES WERE UPROOTED. THE PATH WAS APPROXIMATELY 0.4 MILES LONG AND 40 YARDS WIDE.

A WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF WINFIELD. THIS LOCATION WAS CLOSE TO THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 27 AND COUNTY ROAD 69. THE TORNADO WAS CAPTURED ON FILM BY MORE THAN ONE CITIZEN. NO DAMAGE WAS REPORTED. SEVERAL ADDITIONAL FUNNEL CLOUD REPORTS WERE RECEIVED ASSOCIATED WITH THIS FEATURE NOT ON THE GROUND.
